results.teachingGoal: Learn the basic route: Power -> Relay -> Core.
Primary push chains through R1; backup P2 is weaker (INJ 3) but can wake R2 directly for steady core feed.
Click the Firewall to open a route before trying to charge the Core.
P1 is the heavy injector; P2 is a lighter support feed. Use both to keep relay branches awake and stack core charge.
Rotate a firewall between two branches and choose the safer route.
Use the gate to decide between a short direct route and a slower relay lane.
Coordinate two power inputs through one gate and keep both branches useful.
Finish with every non-virus node active, not just the core charged.
Intro virus pressure: firewall modes swap route targets, so read mode/output labels before corruption spreads.
Clean infection while advancing core charge; virus can re-infect relays on alternate turns if purifier/flow pressure drops.
Clean the corrupted power source, then take the safe branch before virus pressure grows.
Two virus fronts force you to route quickly before the budget runs out.
Spot the dangerous route and avoid feeding the overload node too hard.
Split feed between the main injector and backup tap so activate_all does not force one script.
Use the firewall like a pressure valve to keep the overload route safe.
Balance two power sources across separate overload nodes without blowing the budget.
Use the firewall to decide when to burst through overload and when to take the safer relay path under virus pressure.
Split power across a gate lane and a virus-pressured relay lane before collapse catches up.
Stabilize the corrupted source, then route through a clean relay lane before virus pressure compounds.
Final exam: cleanse the source, choose between overload burst and safe relay routing, and finish the district core.
Use two power taps in sequence before committing to the relay lane.
Switch between safe relay output and overload burst timing.
Alternate between safe relay output and risky overload burst under virus pressure.
Clean the corrupted relay, then use a backup injector to finish both branches cleanly.
Route energy through the purifier lane to stabilize an infected relay while charging the core.
Choose between direct core feed and purifier support to control virus pressure.
Keep purifier support online to finish charge while clearing infection; P2 is a low-strength support injector, not a burst lane.
Balance firewall routing between overload burst and purifier-backed stability.
Use a firewall gate to decide when purifier support outranks raw relay throughput.
Choose between overload burst and purifier cleanse windows while finishing the core objective.
Rotate firewall modes (including dual-output mode) to keep purifier support online while cleansing and routing through relay chains.
Alternate firewall routes to keep overload under control while purifier support preserves full activation.
Coordinate dual sources so purifier upkeep and core throughput do not fight each other.
Balance route activation and full sanitation in the same run under two-lane pressure.
Chain two firewall decisions so purifier cleanup and overload lanes stay synchronized.
Final gauntlet: firewall routing must balance overload burst with purifier-backed sanitation.
Feed one splitter to power two relay lanes in the same turn.
Choose between a direct overloaded lane and a safer but weaker splitter lane.
Keep purifier coverage online across multiple turns so clean_corruption and core charge advance together beyond the opener.
Alternate firewall lanes so splitter support can stabilize virus pressure while overload remains useful.
Prime the breaker before feeding an overload lane, or the route blows out.
Raw pushing overloads the lane; time a breaker prime to keep a final feed safe.
Complete activate_all without blowing the overload lane by timing breaker primes.
Chain firewall lane control with splitter support; some modes are safe setup while others expose the overload burst lane.
Prime breaker bursts while purifier pressure clears a corrupted relay lane; raw pushes can overheat before breaker timing is set.
Prime breaker-fed overload bursts while virus pressure degrades the relay backup lane; unprimed bursts are intentionally high risk.
